name: Canada Critical Cyber Systems Protection Act (Bill C-26)
summary: Canada's Critical Cyber Systems Protection Act establishes a cybersecurity framework for federally regulated critical infrastructure operators. Part of Bill C-26, it empowers the government to direct operators to protect their cyber systems, mandates incident reporting to the Canadian Centre for Cyber Security, and creates information-sharing mechanisms across sectors.
keyRequirements: Designated operators must establish and implement cybersecurity programs
Mandatory reporting of cybersecurity incidents to the Canadian Centre for Cyber Security
Governor in Council may issue cybersecurity directions to protect critical cyber systems
Covers federally regulated sectors: telecommunications, finance, energy, and transportation
Record-keeping obligations for cybersecurity programs and incident reports
Information sharing with government agencies for threat intelligence coordination
penalties: Administrative monetary penalties up to CAD 15 million per violation for corporations. Criminal offences up to CAD 1 million fine and/or 5 years imprisonment for individuals who obstruct or provide false information. Compliance orders and injunctions.
